senses.definition: a replica or imitation of a real weapon, typically made from safe and non-harmful materials, designed for play and entertainment purposes, and it should not pose any actual threat or danger when used responsibly

examples.x: The kids were running around the yard, pretending to fight with their toy weapons.
The store sells a variety of toy weapons, from foam swords to plastic guns.
The children enjoyed playing with their toy weapons during their outdoor adventure game.
I bought a toy weapon for my nephew’s birthday because he loves pretend play.

senses.definition: a type of toy that includes moving parts or mechanisms that are operated by manual or automatic means, and these toys often involve gears, springs, levers, or other mechanical systems to create movement and animation

examples.x: The child was excited to see the mechanical toy car zoom across the floor after winding it up.
My grandfather used to have a collection of mechanical toys that he would show me when I was little.
She smiled as she watched the mechanical toy bird flap its wings and chirp.
The mechanical toy train chugged around the track, powered by a small wind-up spring.
He bought a mechanical toy that dances whenever you press the button on its back.

senses.definition: a type of gun that uses compressed air to shoot small round metal balls

examples.x: He used his BB gun to practice shooting at targets in the backyard.
My friend bought a BB gun for his birthday and we spent the afternoon trying it out.
They had a competition to see who could hit the most targets with a BB gun.
I accidentally shot the BB gun and it left a small dent in the fence.

senses.definition: a toy gun that creates a loud popping sound and a small flash using small explosive caps or paper rolls when the trigger is pulled, without firing projectiles

examples.x: He used his cap gun to pretend he was a sheriff in the Wild West.
The kids were running around the yard, firing their cap guns during the game.
I remember having a cap gun when I was younger and pretending to be in a cowboy movie.
The sound of the cap gun popping echoed through the neighborhood during the play.
He pulled out his cap gun and aimed it at the cardboard target.

senses.definition: a type of toy motor commonly found in toy cars and other vehicles, where the motion of the toy is generated by the friction between the wheels and the surface it moves on, without the need for batteries or external power sources

examples.x: The friction motor in this robot makes it move across the table without needing batteries.
The friction motor inside this toy airplane makes it soar when you give it a good pull.
We tested different friction motor toys to see which one could go the farthest.
I showed my nephew how to wind up his friction motor toy and let it go, and he was amazed by how far it went.

senses.definition: a traditional wooden toy with notched edges that, when rubbed or manipulated in a specific way, causes the propeller-like piece to spin and create sound effects, providing a simple and entertaining form of folk toy amusement

examples.x: He spent the afternoon trying to master the gee-haw whimmy diddle, twisting the stick back and forth to make it spin.
The gee-haw whimmy diddle is a fun toy that requires patience and skill to get the spinning motion just right.
As a child, I would often play with a gee-haw whimmy diddle, fascinated by the buzzing sound it made.
The children gathered around to watch the older man demonstrate how to use the gee-haw whimmy diddle at the festival.
My grandfather showed me how to use a gee-haw whimmy diddle when I visited his farm last summer.

senses.definition: a toy gun that shoots small water-absorbent gel balls as projectiles, typically used in recreational games similar to airsoft or paintball, but using harmless and biodegradable gel balls instead of paint or plastic pellets

examples.x: The kids had a blast playing with their gel guns in the backyard, trying to hit each other with the soft gel balls.
After buying a gel gun, he spent the afternoon setting up targets in the garden to practice his aim.
For his birthday, he received a gel gun and a pack of colorful gel balls to use in outdoor games.
The gel gun was a fun alternative to traditional nerf guns because the gel balls didn’t hurt when they hit.

senses.definition: a toy gun that produces a popping sound when a cork or other mechanism inside is released, simulating the sound of a gunshot without actually firing any projectiles, providing a fun and safe play experience for children

examples.x: The children had a blast playing with their pop guns at the park.
He picked up a pop gun at the toy store for his little brother’s birthday.
When I pulled the trigger, the pop gun made a loud sound that startled everyone.
The old wooden pop gun on the shelf reminded me of my childhood.
I found a pop gun in the toy chest while cleaning out the attic.

senses.definition: a toy motor found in vehicles that moves the toy forward when pulled back and released, without using batteries or external power sources

examples.x: I wound up the pullback motor on my toy car, and it zoomed across the floor.
My younger brother loves playing with his pullback motor toys because they don’t need any batteries.
The pullback motor on the little truck works really well; it moves fast after a few pulls.
We used a pullback motor in our model cars for a school project to show how stored energy works.

senses.definition: a toy gun that uses compressed air or gas to launch small pieces of potato or other soft objects, typically used for recreational play and target shooting

examples.x: He showed me how to load the spud gun with a small piece of potato and shoot it across the yard.
We spent the afternoon in the backyard, taking turns firing the spud gun at targets.
The kids had a blast testing their aim with the spud gun at the family barbecue.
The spud gun was a hit at the party, and everyone took a shot to see who could make the longest shot.

senses.definition: a miniature replica of a real car, often made of plastic or metal, and designed for play and entertainment, allowing children to engage in imaginative pretend play and simulate driving experiences

examples.x: The child pushed the toy car across the floor, pretending it was racing in a competition.
He was excited to receive a new toy car for his birthday.
She collected toy cars, each one carefully displayed on her shelf.
My little brother loves to play with his toy cars during his free time.
They spent hours creating a track for their toy cars to race on.

senses.definition: a fake gun used for playing games, and it cannot shoot real bullet

examples.x: She pretended to be a superhero, using her toy gun to save the day.
The toy gun made a loud popping sound every time he pulled the trigger.
My little brother loves playing with his toy gun during our outdoor games.
The child was excited to receive a toy gun for his birthday.

senses.definition: a miniature train model designed for play or as a collectible item, typically running on tracks and providing children with imaginative and fun playtime

examples.x: My daughter loves setting up her toy train with little houses and trees.
I bought my nephew a new toy train for his birthday, and he was thrilled.
The toy train made a chugging sound as it moved across the floor.
He spent the afternoon fixing his old toy train to make it run smoothly again.
We set up the toy train under the Christmas tree as part of our holiday decorations.

senses.definition: a mini version of a real truck, designed for play and fun

examples.x: The children were playing with their toy trucks in the sandbox.
He got a toy truck for his birthday and spent hours driving it around the living room.
They built a little road in the driveway and raced their toy trucks across it.
She used a toy truck to carry all her toy animals from one side of the room to the other.
My little brother loves to play with his toy truck while I do my homework.

senses.definition: a small, wheeled vehicle resembling a miniature version of a real wagon, commonly used for play and transporting toys or objects, often pulled by children or used in imaginative play scenarios

examples.x: The children enjoyed playing with their toy wagon in the backyard, loading it up with stuffed animals.
He pushed the toy wagon down the sidewalk, pretending it was a delivery truck.
My little brother loves pulling his toy wagon around the house, moving his toys from room to room.
The toy wagon was a birthday gift, and the kids couldn’t wait to take it outside to play.
After the picnic, they used the toy wagon to transport their empty snack containers back to the car.

senses.definition: a small, rubber balloon filled with water, commonly used for outdoor water games and activities, providing a fun and refreshing way to cool down during warm weather

examples.x: We had a great time at the park, throwing water balloons at each other.
The kids filled up the water balloons and got ready for the water fight.
It was so hot outside, we decided to cool off by tossing water balloons around.
She quickly dodged the water balloon thrown at her, laughing as it splashed nearby.
I accidentally popped a water balloon while trying to fill it up.

senses.definition: a toy gun that shoots water, typically used for outdoor play and water-based games, providing a fun and refreshing way to engage in water fights and activities during hot weather

examples.x: We brought our water guns to the park for a fun water fight.
The kids spent the afternoon chasing each other around with water guns.
I filled my water gun with cold water to cool off in the heat.
For her birthday, she got a giant water gun that can shoot really far.
They filled the water guns and got ready for the big water battle.

senses.definition: a mechanical toy that requires winding a key or knob to store energy, which is then released to power the toy's movement or action, providing entertainment without the need for batteries or electricity

examples.x: She gave the wind-up toy a twist and smiled as it started walking in circles.
My grandmother gave me a wind-up toy dog for my birthday when I was young.
I found an old wind-up toy in the attic and decided to try it out again.
The wind-up toy bunny hopped around the table every time I turned the key.

senses.definition: a miniature toy car that runs on a track with a slot or groove, using electric power from the track to propel the car forward, and it is controlled by a hand-held controller, providing a fun and interactive racing game

examples.x: I spent the afternoon racing my slot car on the track with my friends.
He bought a new set of slot cars for his birthday and can’t wait to try them out.
My brother and I always have a blast racing our slot cars when we visit our grandparents.
She was thrilled when her slot car zoomed past the finish line in first place.
We set up the slot car track in the living room and had a race competition.

senses.definition: a mechanical plaything designed to imitate the movements and behaviors of real robots, offering interactive and entertaining experiences for users of all ages

examples.x: My little brother loves playing with his toy robot every afternoon.
The toy robot can walk and make sounds when you press the buttons.
She got a new toy robot for her birthday, and it lights up when it moves.
We spent hours playing with our toy robot and making it race across the floor.
